visible_observations: The board presents twelve selected masters in a four-by-three grid, with a three-silhouette strip beneath each master.

The roster spans a massive arch-backed humanoid, an upright shell-like pod, several lean humanoids, a ratfolk claw user, a bat archer, a pale tendril organism, a goblin support figure, a giant-fist brawler, a crab-heart creature, and a long-rifle marksman.

Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna form the tightest visible cluster: each is a tall adult woman in dark fitted garments using an extended open-hand gesture. Cashmere and Quillith also carry similarly sized rectangular occult objects.

Korath, Veyra, Pilfer, Nyxa, Creep, and Malachor have silhouette triptychs with visibly different body or appendage propositions; several humanoid triptychs mostly vary stance, arm direction, or prop placement within one body premise.
strengths: Large body-plan differences make Veyra, Nyxa, Creep, Malachor, Korath, and Sable rapidly separable in the roster view.
The repeated silhouette strips make broad massing and prop-anchor similarities visible at a glance.
defects: A substantial humanoid subset converges on upright proportions, black or dark-brown fitted leather or cloth, exposed upper torsos, and restrained hand-led poses.
Silhouette exploration breadth is inconsistent across the roster: some triptychs change the governing shape while others read as pose variants of the same premise.
The shared dark smoky presentation compresses costume-material and color differences among the humanoid masters.
uncertainties: The contact-sheet scale suppresses fine facial, edge, and surface-detail differences that may be visible in the individual masters.
contamination: Names, role or trait labels, and SELECTED A, B, or C labels are displayed beside the visual evidence.

visible_observations: The enlarged crops range from a mature weary human face through ratfolk, batlike, and goblin faces to Veyra's slit-like head, Creep's faceless alien head, and Malachor's face-absent heart-and-rib form.
Nyxa's exposed teeth and Korath's compressed scowl create stronger facial-shape departures than most of the humanoid crops.

Cashmere, Pilfer, Knoll, Quillith, Kett, Luna, and Sable largely cluster around narrowed eyes with a restrained half-smile, smirk, or tight mouth rather than widely different mouth and brow structures.
Veyra, Creep, and Malachor direct attention through head shape, openings, or anatomy rather than conventional eyes and mouths.
strengths: Species, age, and facial-anatomy variation prevents a single face template from governing the full roster.
The nonhuman entries establish multiple visible mechanisms for attention without relying on a conventional human face.
defects: The human and humanoid expressions repeatedly use narrowed eyes and controlled mouth corners, reducing visible emotional separation within that subset.
Several humanoid crops depend more on grooming, species, or costume context than on distinctly different facial action.
uncertainties: A clean image-only face board is not present in this file, so the face crops cannot be inspected here without adjacent intended-reading text.
contamination: Answer-bearing text beside every crop states the intended private want, primary fear, active survival strategy, current emotion, contradiction, visible face direction, and forbidden read.

visible_observations: Across the twelve crops, the strongest roster-level separations come from the nonhuman head structures and Nyxa's tooth-bearing snarl.
The conventional humanoid subset repeatedly uses direct or three-quarter glamour framing, narrowed eyes, and restrained mouth-corner movement.
Korath's older lined face and Kett's heavier masculine face interrupt the polished adult-female cluster, while Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, and Sable remain comparatively close in presentation.
strengths: Head anatomy ranges from fully conventional human faces to animal, alien, faceless, and body-as-face solutions.
The uniform crop treatment makes repeated humanoid eye and mouth patterns readily comparable.
defects: The adult-human face group has less expression-shape variety than the roster's species and anatomy variety.
Several controlled half-smiles, tight mouths, and narrowed gazes create a recurring composed or sly register.
uncertainties: The adjacent authored psychology descriptions prevent this board from serving as a clean unaided convergence test.
contamination: Answer-bearing intended psychology and forbidden-read text is displayed beside every face crop.

visible_observations: The board places twelve 96-pixel derivatives in a four-by-three comparison grid.

Korath's arched mass, Veyra's leaf-pod contour, Pilfer's amber claws, Nyxa's oversized bow and ears, Creep's tendril halo, Kett's giant fist, Malachor's pincers, and Sable's long rifle remain visible shape anchors at this size.

Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna reduce to similarly tall dark female figures with thin limbs and hand-led gestures; Cashmere and Quillith retain small rectangular side props but lose most prop-surface specificity.
Fine facial action, garment layering, small anatomy costs, and dark-on-dark internal edges are largely unavailable at native scale.
strengths: Large external props and unusual body envelopes survive reduction more consistently than internal texture or facial detail.
The most anatomically distinct nonhuman units remain separable without relying on readable faces.
defects: Dark costumes merge into the dark backgrounds, especially on the upright humanoid units.
Several psychologically specific or costume-specific cues collapse into small low-contrast marks at 96 pixels.
The tall dark female caster subset remains close in mass, value, and pose after reduction.
uncertainties: The board shows isolated derivatives rather than their final gameplay background, neighboring units, motion, or UI context.
contamination: Names, role or trait labels, and PRIMARY labels are displayed beside the 96-pixel images.

visible_observations: At native size, recognition is governed primarily by the outer contour and one oversized prop or anatomy anchor.
The pod, bat-bow, tendril, giant-fist, crab-pincer, rifle, and arched-mass entries remain visually distant from one another.
Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna converge into narrow upright dark figures; Knoll also becomes a dark humanoid column whose small pale paper is more legible than much of the body detail.
The shared smoky backgrounds and subdued dark palette become a stronger roster-level commonality after reduction.
strengths: Distinct body-plan and prop anchors preserve separation for most of the nonhuman and weapon-dominant entries.
The consistent reduction exposes which roster distinctions rely on scale-stable mass rather than fine rendering.
defects: The dark upright humanoid group loses much of its material, expression, and internal-shape differentiation.
Repeated thin-limbed open-hand poses remain visibly close after reduction.
uncertainties: No animated, in-world, or crowded-unit native-size comparison is included in this file.
contamination: Names, role or trait labels, and PRIMARY labels provide authored classification beside each native-size image.

visible_observations: The board groups the twelve masters under Tank, Mage, Assassin, Support, Marksman, and Brawler headings at comparable panel sizes.
The three tank panels use visibly different governing envelopes: a massive arched humanoid, a vertical shell pod, and a wide crab-heart organism.
The assassin pairing separates a compact ratfolk claw silhouette from a tall pale tendril form, and the marksman pairing separates a bat-and-bow mass from an upright rifle silhouette.

The mage pairing places two tall athletic adult women in open-hand spell gestures side by side; Cashmere adds a book and dark coat, while Luna adds an open midriff, color accents, and a diagonal thread-like strand.
The support pairing places two upright dark-clad humanoids with document-like pale or rectangular props and directed hand interaction side by side.
Across groups, dark smoky grounds and black, brown, gray, or desaturated costume values recur more consistently than bright hue separation.
strengths: Tank, assassin, and marksman pairings retain large body-plan or prop differences within the displayed groupings.
Consistent panel treatment makes repeated costume, pose, and value structures visible across roles.
defects: The mage pairing converges on gender, proportions, stance, and hand-led spell presentation.
The support pairing shares upright humanoid massing, dark costume values, and paper or rectangular interaction props.
The common dark palette and smoky backgrounds compress material separation across several humanoid panels.
uncertainties: The board's authored group headings and explanatory text prevent a clean image-only role or intent reading.
contamination: Each master is accompanied by answer-bearing lane, prop or anatomy, visible cost, emotion, and contradiction text.

visible_observations: A large top panel labeled Vellum precedes twelve equal-size Phase 2 master panels.

The Vellum panel uses a silver-haired upright woman, a long layered black robe and corset, multiple curling parchment strips, orange trim, blue fragments, and a quill or staff, producing many external arcs and separated cloth, paper, and metal regions.
Veyra, Nyxa, Creep, Malachor, Korath, Pilfer, and Kett remain visibly distant from the top panel through nonhuman anatomy, very different body mass, or oversized weapon anatomy.

Cashmere and Quillith share the closest broad visual vocabulary with the top panel: dark feminine tailoring, pale skin, and document or occult-object props, but their silhouettes use fewer paper arcs and fewer separated material regions.
Across the twelve lower panels, dark smoky grounds, black leather or cloth, and restrained brown-gray values recur; bright blue, orange, green, or red accents occupy smaller areas on most units.
strengths: The lower roster contains multiple anatomy-led silhouettes that cannot be reduced to the top panel's upright robed humanoid structure.
The common panel scale makes external prop arcs, body envelopes, palette spread, and material separation directly visible.
defects: Several humanoid masters converge on dark tailored leather or cloth, upright stances, and small document-adjacent props.
The lower humanoid panels generally show fewer large separated color and material regions than the top Vellum panel.
Cashmere and Quillith are especially close to one another in dark feminine tailoring, extended-hand pose, and rectangular occult-object placement.
uncertainties: The comparison embeds authored reference framing and intent labels, so it is not a clean unaided image-only roster test.
The board presents reduced master panels rather than each source at its native full resolution.
contamination: The board explicitly labels Vellum as a benchmark or reference, provides instructions about what to compare, and prints lane, emotion, and dominant-cue text with the unit panels.

visible_observations: The comparison shows each complete master from head or top anatomy through the grounding shadow, allowing whole-concept silhouettes and broad material blocks to be compared.
The top Vellum concept separates robe, corset, parchment, metal, hair, and magical fragments through distinct shapes and color accents.
The Phase 2 nonhuman concepts obtain material and edge variety through shell leaves, fur and membranes, pale tendrils, claws, bone-like ribs, chitin, and metal weapons.
Several Phase 2 humanoid concepts instead resolve primarily as one dark garment mass with small skin, paper, metal, or magic accents.

Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, Knoll, Kett, and Sable share upright human-scale presentation, while their main separating anchors are respectively a book, rectangular board, diagonal strand, pale paper, oversized fist, and long rifle.
strengths: Distinct anatomy and oversized props provide clear whole-concept anchors across much of the lower roster.
The panel arrangement exposes differences in silhouette fragmentation, material blocking, and accent-color distribution.
defects: Dark humanoid costume masses frequently merge with the dark presentation grounds.
Some humanoid concepts depend on one prop for separation while retaining similar upright proportions and subdued garment values.
Fine face and surface distinctions are less visible than the large anatomy and prop distinctions at the board's displayed scale.
uncertainties: This composite does not provide native-resolution inspection of individual brushwork, edge control, or small material transitions.
Authored comparison labels sit alongside the visuals and may prime intended readings.
contamination: The file contains answer-bearing reference instructions plus lane, emotion, and dominant-cue annotations rather than presenting unlabelled master concepts.
